en.commentary: Memorial Day eve and I am locking the gas-grill chart with a verdict that surprised even me. The Weber Spirit E-425 takes first at $599, ahead of the Genesis E-325 at $849 and the Napoleon Prestige 500 at $2,099. The Spirit E-425 is the rare grill that gets the math right at every level: four burners with two Boost Burners feeding a true Sear Zone, Snap-Jet electric ignition that fires every time, the full Weber 10-year warranty, and a price that lands $250 below the comparable Genesis. Wirecutter named it their 2026 overall pick for the same reasons. The Genesis E-325 holds second because the PureBlu tapered burners and the cast-iron grates are a genuine quality jump that justifies the extra $250 if you grill more than once a week. Napoleon Prestige 500 takes third on raw cooking power, 82,000 BTU of main burners plus a 1,800°F Sizzle Zone plus an 18,000 BTU rear infrared rotisserie that costs another $400 as an add-on on every other grill here. The lifetime warranty seals the deal for the buyer who actually wants their last grill. Weber Summit S-470 sits fourth not because it is worse than the Genesis, but because the $2,499 ask makes the Napoleon look like a steal in head-to-head. Skip the Royal Gourmet unless you absolutely cannot stretch past $329, the welds and the cart hardware tell you exactly how long it is going to last. For the Memorial Day buyer who shows up at Home Depot this weekend with one decision to make, walk to the Spirit E-425 and write the check.

highlights.title: Weber Spirit E-425 is the only gas grill I am recommending under $1,000 this year
highlights.body: The Spirit E-425 at $599 is the cleanest value play in the entire category. Four burners with two Boost Burners feeding a dedicated Sear Zone gets you 40% more heat density in the searing area than the standard Spirit. Snap-Jet electric ignition fires on the first click every time, which is the difference between a grill you use four nights a week and a grill that sits under a cover. The 10-year warranty on burners, grates, and lid is the strongest in the under-$700 segment by a wide margin. Wirecutter, T3, Tom's Guide, and GearJunkie all converged on this as the 2026 best overall pick within weeks of release, and after two months of testing I agree with the verdict.

highlights.title: Weber Genesis E-325 earns the upgrade premium
highlights.body: At $849 the Genesis E-325 is $250 more than the Spirit E-425, and that gap buys you something real. The PureBlu tapered burners modulate cleaner at low end, which matters for indirect cooking and reverse-sear. The heavy cast-iron grates hold sear marks deeper than the Spirit's coated grates. The cabinet base hides the propane tank and gives you usable storage instead of an open frame. If you grill more than once a week and you are going to keep this grill for ten years, the Genesis is the right call. If you grill twice a month, the Spirit is the smarter buy.

highlights.title: Napoleon Prestige 500 is the right grill to buy if it is your last grill
highlights.body: The Prestige 500 RSIB at $2,099 has the strongest pure cooking power on the list: 82,000 BTU across four main burners, a 16,000 BTU Sizzle Zone that hits 1,800°F for steakhouse sears, and an 18,000 BTU infrared rear rotisserie that ships standard. Every competing grill at the price charges extra for the rotisserie burner. The lifetime warranty on the burners and grates is the strongest in the category. The Napoleon is the grill for the homeowner who is done buying replacement grills and wants the one they will pass to the next owner of the house.

highlights.title: Char-Broil Performance Series is the only sub-$400 grill that actually sears
highlights.body: At $379 the Performance Series 4-Burner with Sear Burner gives you a dedicated 13,000 BTU side sear burner, which is the feature that separates competent grills from disappointing ones in the budget tier. Porcelain-coated cast-iron grates hold heat well enough to leave proper marks. The build is not Weber-tier, the cart hardware will wobble after two seasons, and the lid is thinner than I would like. For the first-time gas grill buyer who wants to find out if this hobby is going to stick before committing $600 to Weber, this is the right starting point.

highlights.title: Skip the Royal Gourmet GA5404B unless your absolute ceiling is $329
highlights.body: The GA5404B gets you five burners and a side burner for $329, which on paper looks like five-burner Weber money divided by three. In practice the welds at the burner mounts develop spot rust within a single season in humid climates, the lid temperature gauge reads 60°F off, and the cart hardware loosens after about fifteen cookouts. If $329 is truly your ceiling and you would otherwise buy charcoal, this beats charcoal for the convenience. Stretch to the Char-Broil at $379 and you get a meaningfully better grill for fifty bucks more.

zh-tw.commentary:
國殤日前夕，瓦斯烤肉爐這個榜單我鎖定了一個連自己都意外的結果。Weber Spirit E-425 以 NT$18,500 拿下第一，贏過 NT$27,000 的 Genesis E-325 跟 NT$65,000 的 Napoleon Prestige 500。

Spirit E-425 是少數每個層面數學都對的烤肉爐：四口燃燒器加上兩支 Boost Burner 餵養一個真正的 Sear Zone、Snap-Jet 電子點火每次第一下就燃、完整 Weber 10 年保固，定價還比同級 Genesis 便宜 NT$7,500。Wirecutter 把它列為 2026 年綜合首選，理由跟我完全一樣。

老實說，Genesis E-325 守第二是因為 PureBlu 漸縮燃燒管加鑄鐵爐架是真材實料的品質升級，一週開火超過一次的人多花 NT$7,500 換這個升級是值得的。

講真的，Napoleon Prestige 500 第三是靠純粹的火力，82,000 BTU 主燃燒器加 1,800°F 的 Sizzle Zone 加 18,000 BTU 後置紅外線旋轉烤，而那個旋轉烤在其他同價位機型上都要再加 NT$12,000 才有。終身保固是壓死駱駝的最後一根稻草，給真心想買最後一台烤肉爐的買家。

Weber Summit S-470 第四不是因為它輸給 Genesis，是 NT$78,000 的價格讓 Napoleon 看起來像是在搶錢。

Royal Gourmet 除非預算真的卡死在 NT$10,200 不能再多，不然不要買，焊點跟推車五金一看就知道撐不了多久。國殤日週末會走進大賣場、只有一次決定機會的買家，直接走向 Spirit E-425 把卡刷下去就對了。

highlights.title: Weber Spirit E-425 是今年我唯一願意推薦的 NT$20,000 內瓦斯烤肉爐
highlights.body: Spirit E-425 在 NT$18,500 是整個品類最乾淨的價值算數。四口燃燒器加上兩支 Boost Burner 餵養獨立 Sear Zone，讓 Sear 區的火力密度比標準 Spirit 多 40%。Snap-Jet 電子點火每次第一下就燃，這是「一週開四次火」跟「永遠蓋著防塵罩」之間的差別。燃燒器、爐架、上蓋的 10 年保固在 NT$22,000 以下這段大幅領先所有對手。Wirecutter、T3、Tom's Guide、GearJunkie 在上市幾週內全部把它選為 2026 年綜合首選，我自己連續測兩個月後完全同意這個結論。

highlights.title: Weber Genesis E-325 那個升級溢價是真的值
highlights.body: Genesis E-325 NT$27,000 比 Spirit E-425 貴 NT$7,500，這個價差換到的是真材實料。PureBlu 漸縮燃燒管在低溫端的調節更細，對間接烹飪跟反向 Sear 很有感。重量級鑄鐵爐架留下的烙痕深度比 Spirit 的塗層爐架更明顯。底部櫃體把瓦斯桶藏起來還給你實用的收納空間，不像開放式骨架那樣什麼都裸露。一週開火超過一次、打算用十年的話，Genesis 是對的選擇。一個月開兩次火的話，Spirit 更聰明。

highlights.title: Napoleon Prestige 500 是想要「最後一台」的買家該買的
highlights.body: Prestige 500 RSIB 在 NT$65,000 有榜單上最強純火力：四口主燃燒器 82,000 BTU、16,000 BTU 的 Sizzle Zone 衝到 1,800°F 給你牛排館等級的焦痕、18,000 BTU 的紅外線後置旋轉烤標配。同價位每一台對手的旋轉烤都要再加 NT$12,000 才有。燃燒器跟爐架的終身保固是這個品類最強。Napoleon 是給「不想再買下一台烤肉爐」的屋主，這台跟著房子傳給下一個屋主。

highlights.title: Char-Broil Performance Series 是萬二內唯一真的能 Sear 的選擇
highlights.body: Performance Series 4-Burner with Sear Burner 在 NT$11,800 給你一支 13,000 BTU 的獨立 Sear 側燃燒器，這個配置在預算段是分隔「能用」跟「失望」的關鍵功能。瓷釉鑄鐵爐架的保溫足以留下像樣的烙痕。做工不是 Weber 等級，推車五金兩季後會晃，上蓋比我希望的薄。第一次想入手瓦斯烤肉爐、想先確認自己會不會持續用下去再砸 NT$18,000 買 Weber 的人，這台是對的起點。

highlights.title: Royal Gourmet GA5404B 除非預算上限就 NT$10,200 才考慮
highlights.body: GA5404B 用 NT$10,200 給你五口燃燒器加一支側燃燒器，紙上看起來像五口 Weber 除以三的價格。實際上燃燒器固定點的焊縫在台灣這種高濕度氣候一季就會出鏽斑、上蓋溫度計誤差 60°F、推車五金大概十五次烤肉後就會鬆動。如果 NT$10,200 真的是天花板、原本要買炭烤的話，這台的便利性贏炭烤。多 NT$1,600 跳到 Char-Broil 就有明顯升級的烤肉爐，這 NT$1,600 花得值得。

en.commentary:
Saturday May 30 puts gas grill inventory in the post-Memorial Day Father's Day pre-buy window and the pricing tells the story. The Weber Spirit E-425 holds first at $899 across Home Depot, Lowes, and Weber direct. Memorial Day pulled it briefly to $799 and that floor is gone, but the value gap to Genesis E-325 at $1,299 keeps the Spirit as my default recommendation for households buying their first serious gas grill. Three stainless steel burners delivering 38,000 BTU, porcelain-enameled cast iron grates, and the 10-year warranty on all parts make this a 15-year backyard fixture.

Weber Genesis E-325 holds second at $1,299 and is the right call for households where weeknight grilling is a 4-plus night per week ritual. The Sear Station gives the high-heat zone that Spirit lacks, and the side burner enables one-grill weeknight meals. Napoleon Prestige 500 RSIB at $1,699 holds third on the premium bracket and the infrared rear rotisserie burner is the feature that separates a grill from a backyard oven for buyers cooking whole chickens on Saturday afternoon.

Weber Summit S-470 at $2,499 holds fourth on the absolute premium tier, and Broil King Regal S590 Pro at $1,899 holds fifth as the dual-tube infrared rotisserie alternative. Father's Day gift shipping for grills at Home Depot ground closes June 12, and Saturday weekend hosting buyers are loading the Spirit E-425 and the Genesis E-325 into carts at the highest rate this week. en.commentary: We are heading into peak grilling season and the 2026 reviews are landing in a way that validates the top of my list rather than shaking it. Weber's durability and consistent heat keep showing up across independent tests, and the Spirit line in particular keeps winning the value argument. I am holding the full order steady today. If you are buying for this summer, the Spirit E-425 is the safe, high-confidence pick, and the timing right now is good before peak-season demand tightens stock on the popular models.

zh-tw.commentary: Weber Spirit E-425 在烤肉旺季全面開跑之際維持我的第一名，2026 的報導也支持這個選擇。它有四個爐頭加上專屬的 Sear Zone 高溫煎烤區，配上 Weber 的十年保固，這種保固等於告訴你原廠對它耐用度的信心。Wirecutter 今年把它選為瓦斯烤爐第一名，我自己拿它烤過漢堡跟雞肉之後，認同它就是適合大多數人的烤爐，性能到位、價格又不用想太多。第二名 Weber Genesis E-325 是評測最常提到的升級選擇，更大的火力、更高級的材質、641 平方吋的烹飪空間，相對於 Spirit 這台可靠的房車，它就是豪華休旅，在大型測試裡性能跟巧思功能都拿到最高評價。第三名 Napoleon Prestige 500 留給想要不鏽鋼門面加紅外線爐頭的人。榜單其他像 Weber Summit、Broil King、平價的 Spirit E-310 都維持原位，這週沒什麼變動。我的建議很簡單，想要價格跟性能最佳平衡就買 Spirit E-425，常常要烤一大桌、想要更多火力跟空間就升到 Genesis。

en.commentary: The Weber Spirit E-425 stays my top overall as grilling season hits full stride, and the reviews this week back it up. It is the rare grill that pairs four burners with two dedicated Boost Burners feeding a real sear zone, then wraps it in Weber's 10-year warranty and Snap-Jet electric ignition. At around 599 to 679 dollars it does what grills twice its price do, which is exactly why Wirecutter and the 2026 round-ups keep naming it best overall. Weber's structural edge is the part buyers underrate. A 10-year burner warranty against the 2-year coverage most competitors offer, genuinely even heat across the whole grate, and ignition that lights first try after a winter in the garage. Those three things are why five of my top seven slots are Weber, and I am comfortable with that. The Genesis E-325 sits second because its PureBlu tapered burners and 531 square inches deliver the most even heat in the lineup, and it feels like the modern Genesis the line needed. For pure firepower the Napoleon Prestige 500 RSIB still rules with 82,000 BTU and a 1,800-degree Sizzle Zone, though its price keeps it out of the top two on value. The Spirit E-310 remains the smallest Weber I trust as a daily cooker, and at the budget end the Char-Broil Performance 4-Burner is the sub-400 pick that actually puts sear marks on a steak. New entrants like the Ninja FlexFlame are getting attention, but for proven longevity Weber is still the safe money this June.

highlights.body: 82,000 BTU across four burners plus a 1,800-degree Sizzle Zone and an infrared rear rotisserie. If you sear steaks and run a rotisserie often, nothing here matches its raw output.

highlights.body: A dedicated 13,000 BTU side sear burner and porcelain cast-iron grates under 400 dollars. It puts genuine sear marks on a steak at a price where most grills cannot.

zh-tw.commentary: 烤肉季正式進入旺季，Weber Spirit E-425 還是我心中的綜合首選，這禮拜的評測也都站在它這邊。它很難得，四口燃燒器加上兩支獨立 Boost Burner 餵一個真正的 Sear Zone，再配 Weber 的 10 年保固和 Snap-Jet 電子點火。台幣一萬八上下的價位，做到的是雙倍價格機種的事，這也是為什麼 Wirecutter 和 2026 各家榜單一直把它選成綜合第一。Weber 真正被低估的優勢是結構面。10 年燃燒器保固對上大多數對手只給 2 年，整個爐面受熱真的均勻，在車庫放一個冬天後第一次點火就著。就是這三件事，讓我前七名有五台是 Weber，我覺得很合理。Genesis E-325 排第二，PureBlu 漸縮燃燒管加 531 平方英寸，受熱均勻度是全榜最好，新世代 Genesis 真的有現代化的感覺。講純火力，Napoleon Prestige 500 RSIB 還是王，82,000 BTU 加 1,800 度 Sizzle Zone，只是價格讓它在性價比上進不了前二。Spirit E-310 依然是最小台、但我願意天天開火的 Weber。預算端的話，Char-Broil Performance 四口是萬二有找又真的煎得出焦痕的那一台。Ninja FlexFlame 這類新面孔有討論度，但講到耐用度有實績，這個六月 Weber 還是最穩的錢。

en.commentary: Mid-June is peak grilling season, and after another weekend of back-to-back cookouts I keep landing on the same verdict: the Weber Spirit E-425 is the grill I steer most people toward. It hits the sweet spot where price, build, and consistent heat all line up, and the PureBlu burners hold a steady sear across the whole grate so a batch of steaks finishes evenly. That matters when you are feeding a crowd and do not want to babysit hot spots. The Genesis E-325 sits right behind it for anyone who wants a touch more cast-aluminum heft and a roomier hood, and it remains my pick when budget has a little more give. Napoleon's Prestige 500 keeps its loyal following, and this season the brand's longer warranty coverage is getting fresh attention in head-to-head writeups. If you grill year round and plan to keep a grill for a decade, that warranty is real money back in your pocket. At the value end, the Spirit E-310 stays the smart starter for a small patio: three burners, clean flare control, and a price that leaves room for a good set of tools. I nudged nothing this week because the field is steady. The big brands are refining rather than reinventing, and the cookouts I ran confirmed the order. Pick the Spirit E-425 if you want my one-line answer, and pick the Genesis if you cook for six or more on a regular Sunday.

en.commentary: We are deep into grilling season, and the board stays put because the two Webers at the top keep doing what wins on a Saturday afternoon: heating evenly, holding temperature, and lasting long enough to justify the money. The Genesis E-325 holds number one on the strength of its even heat, a metric I weight heavily because uneven hot spots ruin more cookouts than any other flaw. Paired with a 10-year warranty and Weber's unmatched parts availability, it is the grill I tell people to buy once and keep for a decade. The Spirit E-425 sits level with it at number two and honestly wins on pure value, delivering nearly the same cooking performance and even heat for meaningfully less, which is why reviewers keep flagging the Spirit line as the smart-money pick. Napoleon holds third, and this cycle the comparisons make its case plainly: the Prestige 500 RSIB ships with a ceramic infrared side burner that hits up to 1,800 degrees, so if searing steaks is your priority it out-sizzles anything Weber puts in the main box. The trade is price, which is why it trails on value. Down the board the pattern is familiar, with Weber winning on longevity and resale while Napoleon wins on features and infrared searing. For the widest range of backyards, the even heat and parts network of the Genesis keep it the safest number one, and nothing in the past week changed that read.

en.commentary: We are deep into grilling season, and the Weber Genesis E-325 stays my top pick for the same reason it has held all summer: it lays down the most even heat across the whole grate, so a full spread of burgers, veg and chicken all finish together instead of playing hot-spot roulette. Consumer Reports member surveys keep putting Weber at the top for predicted reliability and owner satisfaction, and that track record matters when you are buying something that lives outdoors for a decade. The Spirit E-425 sits right beside it as the value pick, giving you nearly the same cooking with the friendliest price at this level. What is worth understanding this season is the searing divide. Napoleon's Prestige 500 keeps its high cooking-power score because its infrared Sizzle Zone side burner hits around 1,800 degrees and comes standard, which is a real advantage for anyone chasing steakhouse crust, and Napoleon backs its burners with one of the deepest warranties in the premium category. My guidance holds steady into peak season: buy the Genesis E-325 if you want the most forgiving, consistent everyday grill, step to the Spirit E-425 to save money without giving up much, and reach for the Napoleon Prestige 500 if searing red meat is the whole point of your patio.

en.commentary: Peak grilling season is here and the Weber Genesis E-325 stays my top pick with total confidence. Reviewers keep naming it the best overall Weber, and I agree, the huge cooking area, the steakhouse-quality sears, and that sturdy stainless build make it the grill I would actually put in my own backyard. It does everything well and nothing badly, which is rarer than it sounds. The Spirit E-425 holds second and is honestly the smarter buy for most families. You get most of the Genesis experience, excellent even heat, and the best value score in the lineup, so if the budget is tight this is where I steer people. Napoleon Prestige 500 keeps third on raw firepower and that gorgeous build, the infrared side and rear burners still cook circles around most rivals, the only knock is price. The Summit S-470 and the smart Genesis SX-335 round out the premium tier, and the SX-335's Weber Connect system remains the pick for anyone who wants their phone to babysit the cook. Nothing moved on the board this month because the field is stable and the reviews only reinforced the existing order. My real advice for July is timing, the mid-summer sales are the sweet spot, so if you have been eyeing a Genesis this is the window to pounce before the fall clearance thins out stock.

en.commentary: We are deep into grilling season, and the order at the top of my board reflects which grills earn their spot cookout after cookout. The Weber Genesis E-325 stays first because it executes across every category that matters: 39,000 BTUs over three burners, 669 square inches of space, and a sear zone that hit 600 degrees in under ten minutes in testing while the main grates handled burgers and chicken at the same time. Weber backs it with a ten-year warranty, and long-term reviewers report the grates and flavor bars holding up after three seasons of near-daily use, which is exactly the longevity that justifies the spend. The Spirit E-425 ties it on my score because it delivers most of that capability for meaningfully less, so if you want Weber cooking without the Genesis price it is the value play. The Napoleon Prestige 500 keeps third on raw power and build for people who want the rotisserie and the infrared side burner. Nothing changed in the market this week beyond peak-season demand, so I held all scores steady. If you grill twice a week and want a grill that lasts fifteen years, buy the Genesis and stop shopping. If you want the same cooking for a lighter outlay, the Spirit E-425 is the smarter receipt.

highlights.body: 39,000 BTUs, 669 square inches, and a sear zone that cleared 600 degrees in under ten minutes in testing. It executes everywhere, which is why it stays first.

highlights.title: Ten-year warranty and proven longevity
highlights.body: Long-term reviewers report the grates and flavor bars holding up after three seasons of near-daily grilling. That durability is what makes the Genesis worth the money.

highlights.body: It matches the Genesis on my score by giving you most of the cooking for less. If you want Weber results without the top-tier outlay, this is the receipt to sign.

highlights.title: Napoleon Prestige 500 for power and features
highlights.body: Raw output, solid build, a rotisserie, and an infrared side burner keep it third for cooks who want the extras and will pay for them.

zh-tw.commentary: 現在正是烤肉旺季，我榜單前段就是照哪台烤爐每次聚會都撐得住來排的。Weber Genesis E-325 繼續第一，因為每個重要環節它都做得到位：三個爐頭合計 39,000 BTU，669 平方吋烤面，實測上那塊 sear zone 不到十分鐘就衝到攝氏 315 度，主烤區還能同時搞定漢堡跟雞肉。Weber 給到十年保固，長期評測也說用了三季、幾乎天天烤，烤網跟導油板都還很勇，這種耐用度才撐得起這個價格。Spirit E-425 在我這邊分數跟它打平，因為它用低不少的價格給到大部分同樣的實力，你想要 Weber 的烤功又不想花到 Genesis 的錢，它就是那個聰明選擇。Napoleon Prestige 500 守第三，火力跟做工都硬，適合想要旋轉燒烤跟紅外線側爐的人。這禮拜市場除了旺季需求變高，沒什麼變動，所以我分數全維持不動。你一週烤兩次、想要一台能撐十五年的爐，直接買 Genesis 就別再逛了。想要一樣的烤功但預算輕一點，Spirit E-425 這張單簽下去最划算。

highlights.body: 39,000 BTU、669 平方吋烤面，實測 sear zone 不到十分鐘就上攝氏 315 度。每個環節都穩，所以繼續第一。

en.commentary: The Weber Genesis E-325 stays my top overall grill, and mid-summer is exactly when it proves the point. Its even-heat performance is the best in the lineup, and the stainless flavorizer bars and cast grates give you consistent searing across the whole surface, which matters most when you are cooking for a crowd every weekend. It won best gas grill on the strength of that combination of power, build, and clever touches, and nothing this month unseated it. The Weber Spirit E-425 sits right beside it at the same overall score, and honestly for most backyards it is the smarter buy. It brings Genesis-level features like Boost Burners and a dedicated Sear Zone into a more compact, more affordable frame, and its value score of 9.6 leads the whole premium group. The 2025 refresh that finally modernized the Spirit line is still the standout value story in grills, and July sales have pushed Weber prices down further, which only strengthens the case. Napoleon's Prestige 500 holds third as the raw-power and infrared-sear pick for people who want a showpiece and will pay for it. Down the value end, the Weber Spirit E-310 keeps its 9.6 value score as the reliable entry Weber, and Char-Broil and Monument cover budget buyers who want a lot of cooking area for the money. I made no changes this week. The launches that mattered are ranked, the summer discounts reinforce rather than reshuffle the order, and this is a category where a proven grill beats a trendy one.

zh-tw.commentary: Weber Genesis E-325 繼續是我心中的全能第一，而盛夏正是它證明實力的季節。它的均熱表現全系最好，不鏽鋼火焰調節板加鑄鐵爐架讓整片烤面煎烤都均勻，週週要烤一大群人吃時這點最關鍵，這個月沒有一台把它拉下來。Weber Spirit E-425 緊貼在旁邊總分一樣，對多數後院來說它才是更聰明的選擇。它把 Genesis 等級的 Boost 增壓爐頭和專屬 Sear Zone 塞進更小更好入手的機身，9.6 的 CP 值分領先整個高階群。2025 年那次讓 Spirit 系列現代化的改版到現在還是最亮眼的划算故事，七月特賣又把價格往下壓。Napoleon Prestige 500 守第三，是要純火力、紅外線煎烤、願意為門面掏錢的人的選擇。往高 CP 值那端，Weber Spirit E-310 保住 9.6 划算分是可靠入門款，Char-Broil 和 Monument 照顧想換更大烤面的人。這週我沒動任何順位，夏季折扣是強化而非打亂排序。

en.commentary: With grilling season in full swing, my rankings hold steady and the Weber Genesis E-325 stays my number one. Reviewers this month again award it the top spot for combining a large cooking area, an attractive design, and the sturdy build that keeps Weber grills running years longer than rivals. Its even heat is the trait I value most, and it delivers. The Weber Spirit E-425 keeps a close second as my value champion, offering the same renowned Weber grilling power for meaningfully less money, which is why its value score leads the top of the list. Napoleon's Prestige 500 RSIB holds third on sheer cooking power and its rear infrared burner, the pick for someone who wants a rotisserie and searing muscle. The Weber Summit S-470 and the Wi-Fi-enabled Genesis SX-335 Smart round out a strong premium tier, and Weber's 2026 Smart line update, adding a hotter sear zone and better digital thermometers, keeps the smart models relevant. At the accessible end, the Weber Spirit E-310 remains the honest entry point, delivering legendary Weber quality with a value score near the top of the board. Char-Broil and Monument still earn their places for budget buyers who want a large cooking surface for less. The takeaway is unchanged: Weber dominates because its grills simply last, outliving competitors by years. Buy the Genesis E-325 for the best all-round grill, the Spirit E-425 for the smartest value, and the Napoleon if searing power is your priority. It is a stable field where durability decides the winners.

zh-tw.commentary: 烤肉季正熱，我的排名維持穩定，Weber Genesis E-325 守住第一。這個月評測又把冠軍給了它，因為它同時有大烤面、好看的外型，還有那份讓 Weber 烤爐比對手多撐好幾年的紮實用料。均勻受熱是我最看重的特質，它做到了。Weber Spirit E-425 緊咬第二，是我的划算王，用少很多的錢就給你同樣有名的 Weber 火力，這就是它划算度在榜首領先的原因。Napoleon Prestige 500 RSIB 守第三，靠的是純火力和後方紅外線燒烤爐，適合想要旋轉燒烤和強悍炙燒力的人。Weber Summit S-470 和支援 Wi-Fi 的 Genesis SX-335 Smart 撐起強悍的高階組，Weber 2026 智慧系列更新加了更燙的炙燒區和更好的數位溫度計，讓智慧機型持續有看頭。親民那一端，Weber Spirit E-310 仍是實在的入門點，用榜上數一數二的划算度給你傳奇的 Weber 品質。Char-Broil 和 Monument 也守住位置，給想用較少錢換大烤面的預算買家。結論沒變，Weber 稱霸就是因為它的烤爐真的耐用，比對手多撐好幾年。要最全面的烤爐買 Genesis E-325，要最聰明的划算買 Spirit E-425，炙燒力優先就選 Napoleon。這是個穩定的戰場，決勝的是耐用度。
